description: SkillTypeAndLevel_CanBeUsedTogether -- displayName.Should().NotBeNullOrEmpty() is the only assertion on the display name value. Should verify exact expected value.
suggestedFix: Assert .Be() with the expected display name instead of .NotBeNullOrEmpty().

description: AllSkillTypesAndAllSkillLevels_CanBeCreated -- loops over 160 combinations but only asserts .NotBeNullOrEmpty(). Would pass even if all entries mapped to the same wrong string.
suggestedFix: Assert specific expected values or at minimum assert uniqueness across levels.

description: SkillType_MembersHaveCorrectNames -- uses .Contain() (subset check) instead of .BeEquivalentTo(). Would pass if extra unexpected members were added.
suggestedFix: Use .HaveCount().And.BeEquivalentTo() pattern like other test files.

description: Missing boundary test -- No test for GetDisplayName with an invalid SkillLevel (e.g., (SkillLevel)0 or (SkillLevel)21).
suggestedFix: Add test for invalid SkillLevel input to document expected exception behavior.

description: GetDisplayName_LowerLevels_SuggestWeakness -- uses .Contain('non') and .NotContain('excellent') which are vague substring assertions.
suggestedFix: Assert exact expected display name with .Be().

description: GetDisplayName_UpperLevels_SuggestExcellence -- asserts .NotContain('poor') which passes for any non-'poor' string.
suggestedFix: Assert exact expected display name with .Be().

description: Individual Has*Member tests use .BeTrue() on a boolean, losing failure context. All redundant with AllMembersAreExpected which uses .BeEquivalentTo().
Same redundant .BeTrue() pattern on individual Has*Member tests for Formation, Tactic, and TeamAttitude. Each group already has an AllMembersAreExpected test.
